factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced framework deal with Iran, canceling military strikes and signaling weekend signing. This removes the primary safe-haven bid that drove gold to $4,250+, creating near-term headwind despite longer-term relief narrative.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY rallied to 99.80 on hot US PPI data and Middle East uncertainty (FXStreet, 02:42 UTC). Stronger USD directly competes with gold pricing and pressures XAUUSD—the inverse correlation is critical here.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit 10y TIPS data in newsflow, but hot US PPI (May wholesale prices above consensus per FXStreet 01:56 UTC) implies real yields may have risen slightly, weighing on gold. This is the single largest structural headwind.

factors.name: Inflation Data (US PPI)
factors.summary: US May PPI came in hotter than expected (FXStreet 01:56, 02:42 UTC), lifting USD and tempering expectations for near-term rate cuts. Higher inflation prints typically sell gold in immediate aftermath as they reduce real-yield appeal.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment Shift
factors.summary: Equity markets surged (Nikkei +4%, Kospi +8%, Al Jazeera 00:47) on Iran deal hopes, indicating risk-on rotation away from safe havens. Gold's safe-haven premium is compressing as equities rally.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI dropped to $85/bbl (from ~$90+) on deal optimism (Bloomberg 22:03, FXStreet 01:59). Lower oil removes inflation hedging argument and signals deflation expectations, structurally headwind for gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (RBA, BoK)
factors.summary: RBA widely expected to hold at 4.35% on June 16 per Reuters poll (Investinglive 03:04). South Korea's BoK governor flagged potential rate hikes as early as next month (Investinglive 01:06). Mixed signals, no immediate directional impact.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike
factors.summary: ECB raised rates for first time since 2023, supporting EUR/USD strength near 1.1575 (Bloomberg 00:03, FXStreet 00:06). This eases some USD pressure and supports gold via softer real-rate expectations abroad.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No explicit GLD/IAU flow data in newsflow. Bloomberg noted gold held gains but made no mention of institutional repositioning; Indian/Chinese seasonality not yet a driver in June.

factors.name: BoJ Policy & Yen Carry
factors.summary: BoJ monetary policy meeting June 15-16 proceeds as planned despite Governor Ueda's hospitalization (FXStreet 01:21). USD/JPY near 160.25 on hot PPI (FXStreet 01:10). No major hawkish surprise signal yet; carry trade remains intact but not aggressively unwinding.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +2.94% in 24h to $4,224 on Iran deal fears and safe-haven bid, but the move is now in PHASE_2 consolidation as three headwinds collide: (1) hot US PPI data lifting real yields and USD/DXY to 99.80, (2) equity risk-on rotation (Nikkei +4%, Kospi +8%) siphoning safe-haven demand, and (3) oil crash to $85 removing inflation hedging appeal. Trump's Iran deal announcement is real but not yet signed—markets are treating it cautiously (Iran Supreme Leader approval uncertain per Investinglive 23:06). The narrative has shifted from geopolitical premium to macro headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ policy decision + forward guidance, June 15–16 (potential surprise hawkish shift)
RBA decision June 16 + forward guidance (widely expected hold, but tone matters for AUD, risk sentiment)
US inflation data (PCE, Core PCE) — no specific date in newsflow but typically mid-to-late month (major real-yield driver)
Iran deal formal signing (weekend per Trump claim, but unconfirmed; if falls through, sharp re-rally in gold/oil)
US economic data (NFP, ISM, retail sales) — no dates specified but ongoing labor market strength will support hawkish Fed view
Powell/Fed speakers — no near-term schedule in newsflow
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal formally collapses or Supreme Leader rejects framework, gold will spike back to $4,250+ on renewed geopolitical premium and risk-off. Conversely, if deal is fully signed AND US economic data continues to support Fed hold/eventual hike, real yields could rise further and push DXY to 100+, crushing gold to $4,100–$4,050. Equities correction would also reverse the risk-on trade and reignite safe-haven bid. BoJ surprise hawkish tilt on June 15 would materially strengthen yen carry unwind and USD, adding downside pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended from $4,150 (pre-spike low) to current $4,224 in 24h; PHASE_2 consolidation suggests pullback entry near $4,200–$4,190 support preferable to chasing into resistance. If Iran deal formally signed, expect further weakness toward $4,180–$4,150; if deal fails, reversal above $4,280 becomes likely. Monitor DXY and real-yield proxies (10y nominal Treasury yield) for next directional signal.
